zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon是William Kyngdon的女兒，於1449年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾勞傑爾斯。她於1474年與John Borlase在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1519年在英國英格蘭可瓦爾享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon是William Kyngdon的女儿，于1449年出生在英国英格兰可瓦爾勞傑爾斯。她于1474年与John Borlase在英国可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1519年在英国英格兰可瓦爾享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: King
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon was born in 1449, in Launcells, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of William Kyngdon. She married John Borlase in 1474, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1519, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon est née en 1449, à Launcells, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de William Kyngdon. Elle a épousé John Borlase en 1474, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1519, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon wurde 1449 in Launcells, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von William Kyngdon geboren. Sie hat 1474 in Cornwall, England John Borlase geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1519 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon è nata il nel 1449 nel Launcells, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di William Kyngdon. Lei sposato John Borlase nel 1474, nel Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1519, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdonは1449年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルラウンセルズでWilliam Kyngdonの娘として生まれた。1474年にイギリスコルンワルでJohn Borlaseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1519年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon(은)는 1449년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 로셀스에서 William Kyngdon의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1474년 영국 코른월에서 John Borlase(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon nasceu em 1449, em Launcells, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de William Kyngdon. Ela casou-se com John Borlase em 1474, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1519, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon родилась в 1449 г. в местности лаунселлс, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали William Kyngdon. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Borlase в 1474 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1519 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Kingdon nació en 1449, en Launcells, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de William Kyngdon. Se casó con John Borlase en 1474, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1519, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años.
